title: Watch: India condoles Khamenei’s death; foreign secretary signs condolence book
summary: ndia has formally condoled the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri visited the Iranian Embassy in New Delhi and signed the condolence book on behalf of the Government of India, conveying sympathies to the Iranian people.

title: Watch: The SIR connumdrum in West Bengal and impact on ensuing assembly polls
summary: The SIR controversy in West Bengal is emerging as a key political issue ahead of the upcoming Assembly elections. As parties trade charges over the implications of the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls, the debate could influence voter perceptions and shape the poll narrative in the state.

title: Days ahead of polls, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal to get new Governors
summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee says Union Home Minister Amit Shah called her and informed about R.N. Ravi’s appointment and that she was “shocked” by the resignation of Ananda Bose

title: Nepal election: Voting concludes with preliminary turnout around 60%
summary: The Election Commission had set up 23,112 polling centres at 10,963 polling stations across 77 districts

title: Iran crisis: 60,000 tonnes of basmati rice stuck in ports, exporters seek urgent govt. aid
summary: Exporters ask Centre to recognise current logistics disruption as a force majeure type; close to 90% of total rice exports to West Asia, worth about ₹25,000 crore annually, is basmati rice

title: Sri Lankan Supreme Court judge moves Karnataka High Court for removal of links of old online news reports from Google
summary: Meanwhile, the High Court asked the advocate for Justice Nawaz to serve notice to Sri Lankan newspapers through emails

title:
Sinking of Iranian ship brings war to region, say 
Sri Lankan and Maldivian leaders
summary: They call for closer communication on the issue with India as a ‘net security provider’ in the region

title: Madras Sanskrit College plans reversion to self-financing institution; students oppose move
summary: The students say they may lose access to events such as the Kasi Tamil Sangam and national-level competitions and seminars; they add that some teachers, who are paid by the Central Sanskrit University, have also planned to leave by March 31, when the tie-up ends
